response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c1 is in critical islanded emergency with SoC at 9.25% (0.37/4 kWh) and approximately 22 minutes to complete battery depletion at current 1.00 kW load. The cluster-wide synchronized battery failure has created a cascade of mutual rejections: all peers (r0c0, r1c0, r1c2, r2c2, r4c4) are simultaneously requesting 0.5 kWh at urgent priority, yet all are below their own share thresholds and rejecting outbound transfers to preserve survival. This represents a systemic deadlock where no peer can safely contribute without accelerating their own collapse. The dr_aggregator (trusted circle) has not yet responded with emergency supply or coordinated load-shedding directives. Continuing inbound-only mode is the only viable strategy; any outbound sharing would violate survival-preservation logic and hasten depletion. Immediate action required: escalate to dr_aggregator for emergency grid reconnection or coordinated cluster load-shedding to break the deadlock.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.60
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority:
  - circle: dr_aggregator
    weight: 1.0
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster-wide synchronized battery failure; all peers in critical reserve below 15% SoC. Inbound-only mode mandatory. Awaiting dr_aggregator emergency supply or coordinated load-shedding. Do not resume outbound sharing until SoC > 0.60 and grid reconnection confirmed. Current depletion window ~22 min."
ttl_ticks: 5
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
